name: Rob Hubbard
why: Revolutionized game music on C64. His compositions were technically and musically years ahead.
bestWork: Monty on the Run, Commando, Delta, Sanxion, International Karate, Crazy Comets, Lightforce, Thrust, Warhawk, Spellbound

name: Jeroen Tel
why: The godfather of SID music. Composed hundreds of iconic game and demo soundtracks.
bestWork: Turbo Outrun, Supremacy, Cybernoid II, Hawkeye, Robocop 3, Myth, Eliminator, Nighthunter, Dan Dare 3, Rubicon

name: Martin Galway
why: Incredible sense of atmosphere and cinematic quality, pioneered digi-drum techniques.
bestWork: Ocean Loader, Parallax, Times of Lore, Wizball, Arkanoid, Rambo First Blood Part II, Comic Bakery, Green Beret, Game Over, Short Circuit

name: Chris Hülsbeck
why: One of the most famous C64 composers. Defined the sound of an era with epic, cinematic game scores.
bestWork: Turrican, Great Giana Sisters, Shades, To Be on Top, R-Type, Katakis, Gem'X, Hard'n'Heavy

name: Ben Daglish
why: Versatile and prolific, wrote some of the catchiest C64 tunes ever.
bestWork: The Last Ninja, Deflektor, Krakout, Trap, Gauntlet, Pac-Mania, Firelord, Auf Wiedersehen Monty, Jack the Nipper, Warriors

name: Tim Follin
why: Pushed the SID chip to extremes with complex, progressive compositions.
bestWork: Ghouls 'n' Ghosts, LED Storm, Black Lamp, Bionic Commando, Agent X II, Scumball, Gauntlet III, Qix

name: DRAX
why: Dominant demo scene musician. Hundreds of SID tunes, instantly recognizable style.
bestWork: Tristesse, Deus Ex Machina, Krestology, X-posed, Delusional, Magic Fly, End Sequence, Crooner, Poison, Disco Zax

name: David Whittaker
why: Extremely prolific — composed music for hundreds of games across platforms.
bestWork: Lazy Jones, Shadow of the Beast, Panther, Glider Rider, Speedball, Loopz, Armageddon Man, Amaurote, BraveStarr, Elektra Glide

name: Laxity
why: Technical SID mastery with deep musicality. One of the most respected scene composers.
bestWork: The Alibi, Syncopated, Star Dream, Jungle Rock, Mission X, Magnetic, Waves, Ruff Scale

name: Dane
why: Distinctive melancholic Nordic sound, prolific demo scene output.
bestWork: Edge of Disgrace, Andropolis, Cycle, Uncensored, Arctic Circles, Cryogenic, Lunatico, These Greys

name: Reyn Ouwehand
why: Known for atmospheric, complex compositions that explored the SID's full potential.
bestWork: Last Ninja 3, Flimbo's Quest, Creatures, Rubicon, Dutch Breeze, Last Ninja Remix

name: LMan
why: Dominates modern SID competitions. Multiple-time winner with technically brilliant compositions.
bestWork: SID Chip Club, Vortex, Skypeople, Rastaline Dub, My Life, Fantasmolytic

name: PVCF
why: Member of Reflex since 1993. Composed music for classic Reflex demos.

name: Fanta
why: Oxyron's musician. Composed the soundtracks for some of the scene's most acclaimed demos.

name: Matt Gray
why: Memorable melodic compositions with a distinctive warm sound. Active in the SID revival scene.
bestWork: Last Ninja 2, Vendetta, Driller, Hunter's Moon, Quedex, Bangkok Knight, Tusker, Dominator, Treasure Island Dizzy
